text: I have just sent the numbers to the Global team.

Thanks for the Q3 numbers, Emmanuele. They look solid. Now the question everyone wants: what do you predict for Q4?
Good question. My projection is positive. I think sales will increase by about eight percent.
Eight percent. Will that be steady, or just one strong month?
Steady, I believe. After the peak in September, the numbers will stay steady, not up and down.
That's good to hear. And the public market? Will it reach the target?
Honestly, I predict a small shortfall there, maybe five percent below the plan. But it will recover in Q1.
Thank you for being clear about that. An honest forecast is more useful than a perfect one.
I agree. So, my estimate: total growth will be strong, and the shortfall will not change the full-year result.
My projection for next quarter is positive.
I think sales will increase by about eight percent.
After the peak, the numbers will stay steady.
We will probably have a small shortfall, but it will recover.
This is only an estimate, but it is my best one.

Hi Emmanuele, David here from the Global team. Quick ask before Thursday's review. This time we don't only want the Q3 results. We want your forecast for Q4. Two numbers, that's all. First, what increase do you predict for the private market? And second, where do you see the public market, on target or a small shortfall? Keep it simple, just your best estimate. Nobody expects a perfect number. We only want to know where you think we will be at the end of the year. Thanks. Talk on Thursday.

Okay, let me wrap up the forecast part. Here is where we landed. For the private market, Emmanuele predicts a steady increase, around eight percent, into next quarter. For the public market, we expect a small shortfall this quarter, but the projection for the first quarter is positive, so it will recover. So, the action points. Emmanuele will send the updated forecast slide by Friday, and we will review the full-year estimate next month. Good work, everyone. The numbers are honest, and honest numbers are the ones we can plan with.

My projection for Q4 is positive. I predict sales will increase by about eight percent. After the peak in September, the numbers will stay steady. The public market will have a small shortfall this quarter. But it will recover in the first quarter, so the full-year estimate is strong.
